ops.body:
HANDOFF (orchestrator picked Option A: fold into the concurrent plugin/hooks rework + ship with its release). Releasing my claim back to the backlog. Authoritative spec for whoever lands this:

(a) MODE-AGNOSTIC pattern per row (a fragment in BOTH the mcp_active and non-mcp message branches — do NOT use the MCP-form fragments, they break CI):
  - record.py:414 (cp -R)    → Warn(pattern="attach")
  - record.py:415 (mv crash) → Warn(pattern="cc-notes log entry")   # unconditional lede ~line 453
  - record.py:417 (Write log) → Warn(pattern="attach")
  - record.py:547 (ephemeral) → Warn(pattern="stores an artifact")
  - record.py:757 (plan)     → Warn(pattern="outlives the session")  # shared by PLAN_TEACH + PLAN_TEACH_MCP, lines 674/690
  Precedent: record.py:632 already ships Warn(pattern="attach") — a mode-agnostic row that passes in both modes.

(b) VERIFY IN BOTH MODES before landing:
  - MCP-active shell: `uvx capt-hook test` from cc-notes (or cc-context) with the cc-notes MCP live → MCP-form messages.
  - Non-MCP isolated run: copy plugin/hooks to /tmp, RENAME the pack in capt-hook.toml so it doesn't shadow the cache, then `uvx capt-hook --hooks /tmp/<copy> test` → CLI-form messages (this is what CI sees).
  CACHE GOTCHA: `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` does NOT test the working tree — a registered pack loads by name from the content-addressed cache ~/.cache/captain-hook/packs/cc-notes@<sha>, so working-tree edits are invisible until committed+pushed+re-resolved.

(c) DOWNSTREAM GATE: cc-context's packs.toml pin @latest resolves to RELEASE TAGS (currently v0.28.0=a231e77c cached as v0.27.0=9e8fb84), not the branch tip. Both released tags carry the stale patterns, so a plain `pack update` keeps the 5 failures. Consumers (cc-context 312/312) see the fix only after a NEW cc-notes release + `uvx capt-hook pack update cc-notes`.

My MCP-form attempt was already swept into and dropped from the rewire commit (4424c703, on main). On-disk record.py is pristine. No further code change from me.
